title: trisetum palustre
intro: Trisetum palustre, also known as marsh fescue, is a perennial grass native to wetland areas in Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of various health issues such as digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant is said to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Trisetum palustre include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be made from the leaves and stems of the plant.
